name: Janis Joplin
title: Last known photos of Janis Joplin, taken from the front row by Peter Warrack at her final concert, Harvard Stadium, August 12, 1970, less than two months before her death.
description: The last known photos of Janis Joplin were taken on August 12, 1970, by amateur photographer Peter Warrack from the front row at Harvard Stadium in Allston, Massachusetts. The show would prove to be her final concert. Performing with the Full Tilt Boogie Band before a crowd of roughly 40,000, Joplin was visibly intoxicated but, in the words of Warrack's partner Kevin McElroy, “even though she was plastered, she nailed it.” Warrack's negatives sat unseen for over 40 years until after his 2008 death, when McElroy and House of Roulx made them public. Less than two months after this performance, on October 4, 1970, Joplin was found dead at the Landmark Motor Hotel in Hollywood from a heroin overdose. She was 27 years old. Her album Pearl, which she was still recording at the time of her death, was released posthumously in January 1971 and produced the hit “Me and Bobby McGee.”

lyrics.text:
Take another little piece of my heart now, baby
Oh, oh, break it
Break another little bit of my heart now, darling
Oh, oh, have a
Have another little piece of my heart now, baby
Well, you know you got it, if it makes you feel good
Oh, yes indeed

bio: Janis Joplin was an American rock and blues singer known for her powerful, raw vocal style. She was one of the most successful and widely known female rock stars of the 1960s counterculture era.
additionalImagesNote: Plates 01, 02 & 03: Harvard Stadium, August 12, 1970, Plate 03 is the original contact sheet from the Warrack negatives. Plate 04: Sunset Sound Recorders, Hollywood, final recording session, October 1, 1970.

location.name: Harvard Stadium, 79 N Harvard St, Allston, MA 02134
